import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
[2]:
data = pd.read_csv("Amazon_BestSelling_Books_500.csv")

[13]:
data["price_category"] = data["Price (USD)"].apply(
lambda x: "Cheap" if x < 15 else "Expensive"
)
sns.countplot(data=data, x="price_category")
[13]:
<Axes: xlabel='price_category', ylabel='count'>
[12]:
data.columns = data.columns.str.strip()
data["Price (USD)"] = pd.to_numeric(data["Price (USD)"], errors="coerce")

# create category
data["price_category"] = data["Price (USD)"].apply(
lambda x: "Cheap" if x < 15 else "Expensive"
)

# plot
sns.countplot(data=data, x="price_category")

plt.show()

plt.ylabel("Number of Books")

plt.show()

[31]:
# now we make a heatmap
# sns.heatmap(gb)
# plt.show() ismeheatmap nhi banyega
[37]:
gb = data.groupby("Sub-Genre")["Reviews"].mean().sort_values(ascending=False)

plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))
colors = ["red","blue","green","orange","purple","pink","brown","black"]
gb.plot(kind="bar", color =colors)

plt.title("Average Reviews by Sub-Genre")
plt.ylabel("Average Reviews")
plt.show()
[40]:
corr = data.corr(numeric_only=True)

plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
sns.heatmap(corr, annot=True, cmap="coolwarm")
plt.show()
[ ]:
# Insight 1
# Books with more weeks on the bestseller list tend to receive more customer reviews (correlation = 0.55).
# Insight 2
# Ratings and reviews have only a weak relationship (correlation = 0.15), suggesting popularity does not always imply higher ratings.
# Insight 3
# Book price shows almost no correlation with ratings or reviews, indicating that expensive books are not necessarily more popular.
# Insight 4
# Higher-ranked books tend to have more reviews, showing a connection between popularity and ranking.

[54]:
top_authors = data.groupby("Author")["Reviews"].mean().sort_values(ascending=False).head(10)

plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))
colors = ["red","blue","green","orange","purple","pink","brown","black"]
top_authors.plot(kind="bar" , color = colors)

plt.title("Top 10 Authors by Average Reviews")
plt.xlabel("Author")
plt.ylabel("Average Reviews")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)

plt.show()
